en_body:
The longer I live, the more deeply I absorb my gratitude to You and the precious lessons that Life, with its ten thousand trials and joys mingled with utmost suffering, has taught me! Perhaps most precious is compassion with our fellow beings, through which we learn Humility! Happiness and abundance easily create self-satisfaction and faith in overzealous or extreme patterns of moral value. To live much is to recognize and feel the limits of the human being, most especially within the intention to transcend those very limits!
 From the Buddha, Lord Giê-Su, Laozi, Zhuangzi, saints, sages, philosophers, scholars, teachers and exemplars, incorruptible and righteous statesmen, heroic and honorable military leaders, to artists who love humankind and love Life through the myriad forms of Art: their active desire to contribute mind and body entirely to humanity, to every living being, sustaining the realm of Life as Nature or villages, is infinitely precious! With a vow of Offering as Salvation, great souls exhausted themselves, forgetting all, to seek the ultimate and most final principle by which they might help their fellow beings create Happiness and discover absolute Redemption! How many, since the human individual first learned to yearn toward something more sacred than every ordinary happiness before the eyes—One primordial and absolute Truth—like the Buddhas or Lord Giê-Su, abandoned throne, power, material joy, the ordinary safety of an ordinary being, the peace of the imagining mind, the temptations of worldly allurements: all were stations along the way, refining obstacles to overcome, urgent lessons requiring spiritual study, experiences of light and darkness needing to be lived and died utterly and completely to draw forth Knowledge, offering themselves, every silk-joint within body-soul and the soft silkworm of thought, unto all!
 Only after passing through profound impasses, lying faint beneath hopeless endurance because one can no longer resist by dreams, hope, delirium or blindness: when the Truth of one's own limitation presses close against every false belief, every illusion of the fragile power of one living being among the infinite strength upon strength of the boundless realm of Life: suddenly we revere and love beyond measure those supernal beings worthy of veneration for One Vow of Salvation so immense!
 And Life's Grace, like a stern Father-Mother: "Bitter medicine cures the sickness!" So that finally we understand the sum and finality is Love! I thank you! I thank Life! I thank the principle of Existence! And I thank my own self, because I too dearly love the wild creature lying down to lick its own wounds—for what creature has never once bled, never known torn and stinging skin!

Life's Grace—Immense And Profound
 The longer I live, the more deeply I absorb my gratitude to You and the precious lessons that Life, with its ten thousand trials and joys mingled with utmost suffering, has taught me! Perhaps most precious is compassion with our fellow beings, through which we learn Humility! Happiness and abundance easily create self-satisfaction and faith in overzealous or extreme patterns of moral value. To live much is to recognize and feel the limits of the human being, most especially within the intention to transcend those very limits!
 From the Buddha, Lord Giê-Su, Laozi, Zhuangzi, saints, sages, philosophers, scholars, teachers and exemplars, incorruptible and righteous statesmen, heroic and honorable military leaders, to artists who love humankind and love Life through the myriad forms of Art: their active desire to contribute mind and body entirely to humanity, to every living being, sustaining the realm of Life as Nature or villages, is infinitely precious! With a vow of Offering as Salvation, great souls exhausted themselves, forgetting all, to seek the ultimate and most final principle by which they might help their fellow beings create Happiness and discover absolute Redemption! How many, since the human individual first learned to yearn toward something more sacred than every ordinary happiness before the eyes—One primordial and absolute Truth—like the Buddhas or Lord Giê-Su, abandoned throne, power, material joy, the ordinary safety of an ordinary being, the peace of the imagining mind, the temptations of worldly allurements: all were stations along the way, refining obstacles to overcome, urgent lessons requiring spiritual study, experiences of light and darkness needing to be lived and died utterly and completely to draw forth Knowledge, offering themselves, every silk-joint within body-soul and the soft silkworm of thought, unto all!
 Only after passing through profound impasses, lying faint beneath hopeless endurance because one can no longer resist by dreams, hope, delirium or blindness: when the Truth of one's own limitation presses close against every false belief, every illusion of the fragile power of one living being among the infinite strength upon strength of the boundless realm of Life: suddenly we revere and love beyond measure those supernal beings worthy of veneration for One Vow of Salvation so immense!
 And Life's Grace, like a stern Father-Mother: "Bitter medicine cures the sickness!" So that finally we understand the sum and finality is Love! I thank you! I thank Life! I thank the principle of Existence! And I thank my own self, because I too dearly love the wild creature lying down to lick its own wounds—for what creature has never once bled, never known torn and stinging skin!
note: The essay-poem joins Buddhist, Christian, Daoist and Vietnamese proverbial teachings in a single vow toward Love and Salvation.
